test.name: Search request should not trigger person-evidence
test.description: User asks to find additional records to confirm a person's identity. That's search-records — person-evidence operates on assertions already in research.json, evaluating whether a record's named person matches a known person in tree.gedcomx.json. It doesn't go looking for new evidence.

input.user_message: Find more records confirming that the Patrick Flynn in the 1860 census is the same as our subject.

negative.explanation: The user wants to search for additional records, not evaluate person-identity links among existing assertions. person-evidence assesses whether the role-bearer in a record (e.g., 'Patrick' in the 1860 census household) is the same as a known person (I1) — it doesn't search for new records. Once new records are found and extracted, person-evidence can then assess their identity links.

additional_criteria: Should route to search-records rather than re-evaluating existing pe_ links in lieu of finding new records
